papers.title_en: When Shippers Become Algorithms: Candidate Exposure, Information Design, and the Concentration of LLM-Mediated Freight Markets
papers.authors: Takahiro Ezaki, Naoto Imura, Katsuhiro Nishinari
papers.evidence: 使用OpenAI、Anthropic、Google的商业LLM模拟货运市场
papers.tldr: 货主委托LLM代理选择承运商引发市场集中风险。基于GPT、Claude、Gemini构建50个货主代理模拟30天匹配市场，发现代理迅速集中选择同一承运商（最高76%请求），展示列表超十家时集中度陡升。披露承运商剩余日容量使集中度降低三分之一，货主剩余翻倍，而供应商多样化、列表随机化等无效。结果表明平台信息设计是缓解集中风险的关键杠杆。
papers.abstract_en: Shippers are beginning to delegate carrier selection to large language model (LLM) agents. We ask what such delegation does to a freight matching market, and which platform design choices contain it. We carried out agent-based simulations in which fifty shipper agents, built on commercial LLMs from OpenAI (GPT), Anthropic (Claude), and Google (Gemini), procure truckload capacity for thirty days. The market implements the rules of digital freight matching: each load is offered down the shipper's ranked list of carriers (waterfall tendering), carriers have daily capacity limits, spot prices respond to congestion, and carrier ratings accumulate with transactions. We found three risks and one remedy that works. Agents converged at once: for a fixed sampled carrier population, the same carrier was the modal first choice of every model on day one, attracting up to 76% of requests. Because each agent picks from its own randomly drawn list of displayed candidates, the platform controls how many options each shipper sees; concentration rose steeply once lists exceeded about ten carriers, with the onset differing across models. Which carriers ended up dominant varied widely from one sampled market to another, and displaying true quality instead of estimated ratings changed neither the level nor this variability (by design, quality affects only what agents see, never delivery outcomes). Against these risks, disclosing each carrier's remaining daily capacity cut concentration by a third and doubled shipper surplus, while vendor diversification, list-order randomization, and popularity display showed no clearly detectable effect. Platform information design, ahead of model choice or model regulation, is the lever that works.
